$comment: The 'validate' feature (custom validation callback on flags) is intentionally excluded from this conformance schema because it is a callable that cannot be serialized to JSON. Both implementations support it (Flag.validate in Python, ValidateFn() in Go) but it is tested via unit tests, not conformance tests.
title: strictcli conformance test cases
description: Array of test cases for verifying strictcli implementations.

skip_schema_validation.description: If true, this test case is exempt from schema validation (used for cases that intentionally craft invalid app definitions).

targets.description: Restrict which implementations run this case. Absent means both. Used for cases that are only expressible in one language (e.g. Python's bad-return hard error, which Go's type system makes unrepresentable).

env.description: Environment variables to set before invoking the app.

config.description: If true, enable config file support and auto-register config subcommands.

config_path.description: Override the default config file path. If null, uses XDG_CONFIG_HOME/<app>/config.<format>.

global_flags.description: Flags declared on the App, parsed before the command name and available to every handler.

checks_path.description: Override CWD-based discovery of checks.toml with an explicit path.

no_default_config_path.description: If true, the app loads no config unless --config is explicitly passed.

config_conflict_mode.description: Conflict resolution mode for config values. In 'error' mode, config+cli and config+env overlaps are hard errors.

infra_root.description: Infrastructure location roots: env var name -> default path. Resolved eagerly at construction; immune to --hermetic.

handshake_env.description: Handshake env vars: env var name -> help string. Read live at access time via the infra accessor.

config_content.description: Raw config file content to write before running the test

config_content_late.description: Raw config file content to write AFTER app construction but BEFORE app.run(). Tests parse-time config loading vs construction-time.

checks_toml.description: Raw TOML content to write as .strictcli/checks.toml for check conformance tests.

checks.description: Check stub definitions for code generation.

providers.description: Check providers registered via register_check_provider / RegisterCheckProvider. Each element is one provider (a list of the specs it returns). Providers have no TOML, so every spec carries its declarative meta inline.

config_fields_def.description: Config field definitions registered on the app via app.config_field() / app.ConfigField().

check.description: A check-impl stub for code generation. The registration form (error vs warn) is NOT specified here -- generators derive it from the check's severity in the embedded checks_toml. This object only describes what the impl mints via its reporter.

mint.description: Which terminal outcome the reporter mints. 'found' requires at least one problem; 'passed'/'skipped' forbid problems.

message.description: The pass/found message or skip reason passed to reporter.passed/found/skipped.

problems.description: Problems the reporter mints before found(). Only valid when mint == 'found'. A warn-severity check may only mint 'warn' problems.

notes.description: Verdict-inert informational notes the reporter records via note()/Note(). They surface only under --verbose and in JSON, on any outcome including a pass.

provider_check.description: A provider-sourced check spec. Unlike a TOML-declared check, it carries all eight declarative meta fields inline (providers have no TOML). The impl body (mint/message/problems) mirrors a 'check' def. 'impl_form' selects the constructor (error_check_spec / warn_check_spec); when it differs from 'severity', it pins the materialization-time severity-mismatch hard error.

impl_form.description: Which constructor registers the spec. Defaults to 'severity'. A value differing from 'severity' pins the severity-mismatch hard error.

mint.description: Which terminal outcome the reporter mints.

notes.description: Verdict-inert informational notes the reporter records via note()/Note().

deprecated.description: If true, this is a deprecated command. It must have deprecated_message and must NOT have handler_prints, handler_exit_code, flags, args, flag_sets, mutex, dependencies, passthrough, or passthrough_handler_prints.

deprecated_message.description: Deprecation message shown when the command is invoked or listed in help. Required when deprecated is true.

handler_prints.description: Template string. {name} is replaced with the value of the arg/flag named 'name'; {source:name} is replaced with the provenance source label via ctx.source(name).

handler_exit_code.description: The integer value the generated handler returns (as outcome(exit_code=N) / Exit(N)). Used as the process exit code.

handler_returns.description: Pins an explicit handler return (survivor-contract cases). When present, the template-printing path is skipped. 'exit' => outcome(exit_code)/Exit(code); 'data' => outcome(data)/ExitData(0,data); 'exit_data' => outcome(exit_code,data)/ExitData(code,data); 'none' => return None (Python)/Exit(0) (Go); 'bad' => an invalid return (Python-only hard error; Go's type system makes it unrepresentable).

data.description: Structured data payload for data/exit_data kinds. Author with sorted keys so compact JSON is byte-identical across languages.

passthrough.description: If true, the command is a passthrough command: no flag/arg parsing, handler receives raw args.

passthrough_handler_prints.description: Template for passthrough handler output. Receives name and raw args. Generated handler prints name:arg1,arg2,...

variadic.description: If true, collects all remaining positional args into a list. Must be the last declared arg.

default_relative_to_root.description: Flag default that resolves through a declared infra root (RelativeToRoot marker).

negatable.description: Whether --no-flagname negation is supported. Only meaningful for bool-type flags.

conflict_mode.description: Per-flag override of the app config conflict mode. Absent means inherit the app-level config_conflict_mode.

stdout_contains.description: Substring(s) that must appear in stdout.

stdout_matches.description: Regex pattern(s) that must match (re.search) somewhere in stdout. For non-deterministic content like durations.

stderr_matches.description: Regex pattern(s) that must match (re.search) somewhere in stderr.

stderr_contains.description: Substring(s) that must appear in stderr.

stdout_not_contains.description: Substring(s) that must NOT appear in stdout.

stderr_not_contains.description: Substring(s) that must NOT appear in stderr.

config_file_contains.description: Substring(s) that must appear in the seeded config file after the run (reads the config_content / config_content_late temp file).

config_file_not_contains.description: Substring(s) that must NOT appear in the seeded config file after the run.

config_file_matches.description: Regex pattern(s) that must match (re.search) somewhere in the seeded config file after the run. Useful for asserting key ordering.
